module_notes: Add notes about this module here.

line_comments.00E9: this is a loop copying the NextDir to BSS.$25
line_comments.0155: If carry set read another character ; C=1 (BLO)
line_comments.0157: else, store filename length in BSS.$13
line_comments.015B: fix the last character of the filename
line_comments.021C: At the end of the cmdlin?
line_comments.0377: Did we reach the next msgBlock?
line_comments.037B: Otherwise copy that char to Y+
line_comments.06A7: B=# of lines, X=location of stuff to print.
line_comments.06AF: Error detected. Break out of loop.
line_comments.06B1: Save the path and line count
line_comments.06B3: Y now contains # chars printed and so does D
line_comments.06B5: Move X pointet to next line
line_comments.06BA: If not loop back where it decrements B for the next line

0011: Initializes BSS, parses command line, opens directory, runs main listing loop.
002D: X points at OS-9 parameter string (does not include program name).
005F: Then detect terminal width if multi-column mode active.
0069: Terminal width detection via I$GetStt subcode $20 (PD.OPT offset in path descriptor).
B returned in X after TFR X,D.
$D0 error = non-CCIO device, also disables columns.
00B6: If AnyFlag set (extended or filter mode), also open CWD separately.
CWD path is the '@'+CR pair at Dat_046C — OS-9 shorthand for current execution dir.
Wait — that's execution dir (@), not current data dir (.).
For -e extended mode, the program needs file descriptor info requiring a second open.
00C9: Initialize column position to terminal width.
If neither extended nor multi-column: skip header, go straight to read loop.
00D3: Write "Directory of " header (15 bytes, no CR — path follows on same line).
00E4: Copy BSS.NextDir path string to BSS.PathBuf.
If pattern flag set (-p), append '/' and pattern after path.
Then I$WritLn the full path — this is the "Directory of /dd/CMDS" header line.
0114: If extended mode: write column headers (dirMsg02) via WritBlines.
dirMsg02 = "User # Last Modified..." header + separator line.
0126: Reads one 32-byte RBF directory entry per iteration.
End-of-directory detected at Loc_0451 via error $D3 from I$Read.
0136: Shift LSN bytes one position down in BSS before reformatting filename.
Reason: FCS→CR reformat may write CR at $75 (BSS.DENend), overwriting LSN0.
After shift, LSN lives at wLSN0/1/2 ($76/$77/$78) — safe from CR overwrite.
The 32-byte I$Read call deposits a 33-byte working structure by design.
013E: $80 → null FCS name (degenerate) — skip length count, go to Loc_014C
if match → dot/dotdot entry — skip (unless -a cleared DotChar)
014C: Count filename length and convert FCS format to CR-terminated string.
FCS = last character has bit 7 set as end-of-name marker.
BCS loop continues while char < $80 (bit 7 clear).
After loop: strip bit 7 from last char, append CR at ,X.
0161: If pattern flag set: call Sub_0317 to wildcard-match filename against pattern.
Sub_035F (called inside Sub_0317) optionally uppercases for case-insensitive compare.
If no match: loop back to $0126 for next entry.
0172: If AnyFlag set (extended or filter mode):
Call I$GetStt on CWD path to get file attributes.
If DirOnly set:  skip non-directory entries.
If FileOnly set: skip directory entries.
01A4: ColWidth=0 → single column mode → branch to $01FB
Compare filename length against remaining column space.
If fits: write filename, pad spaces, update ColmPos.
If doesn't fit: emit CR, reset ColmPos, write filename on new line.
01B0: I$Write the filename (not WritLn — no CR yet, more entries may follow).
Compute padding: subtract filename len from ColmPos in steps of 16.
Write padding spaces from Dat_0519 (19-space pad string).
01DF: Filename didn't fit — start new line.
I$WritLn cwdAndCR (just a CR byte) to terminate current line.
Reset ColmPos to LastCol (full width again).
If BSS.ColFlag set (something was on this line before): loop to pad logic.
01FB: Reached when ColWidth=0 (set by -s, or forced by narrow terminal).
If ExtFlag set → format full extended line via Loc_036E.
If ExtFlag clear → simple I$WritLn of filename (30 bytes).
036E: Copies filename portion from dirMsg03 format template to BSS.PathBuf.
Then fills in metadata fields by reading from file descriptor sector.
Uses wLSN0/1/2 to locate the file descriptor sector.
Field order in output: user#, date, time, attributes, sector, size, filename.
Sub_041A: converts byte to 2 decimal ASCII digits in output buffer.
Sub_042B: converts byte to 2 hex ASCII digits.
Sub_0442: strips leading "space+zero" for cleaner numeric fields.
0451: Error $D3 = end-of-directory (I$Read past last entry) = normal termination.
If multi-column and ColWidth still set: emit final CR to terminate last line.
F$Exit with B = 0 (normal) or error code.
0519: The last line is a format template — fields updated in place.

bss_comments.0: path number for the directory opened for reading
bss_comments.1: path number for CWD (opened when -e flag set)
bss_comments.2: pointer to current directory path string (CR terminated)
bss_comments.4: pointer to end of current token in command line
bss_comments.6: pointer to wildcard pattern string (CR terminated)
bss_comments.10: set when at least one entry written to current output line
bss_comments.13: set by -e or -l: extended listing mode
bss_comments.14: set by -d: show only directory entries
bss_comments.15: set by -f: show only non-directory entries
bss_comments.18: remaining columns on current line — resets to LastCol each new line
bss_comments.19: length of current filename after FCS→CR reformat
bss_comments.21: temp char storage during wildcard pattern matching in Sub_0317
bss_comments.23: I$Open/I$ChgDir mode byte — built from flags
bss_comments.37: output buffer: copy of NextDir path, reformatted for display
bss_comments.88: RBF directory entry buffer (32 bytes from I$Read)
bss_comments.117: byte 28 of dir entry — last char of FCS name OR CR terminator
bss_comments.118: working LSN high byte (shifted from $75 before name reformat)
bss_comments.121: purpose unknown — cleared at init, not seen used after
bss_comments.122: dot-file filter char (init='.', cleared by -a to show all)
